revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 2 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23388260132|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Disputed_inline%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} ==Usage== :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} To link to a centralized discussion on a page '''other than''' the article's [[:en:Talk page|Talk page]], use the {{para|talk}} parameter (a.k.a. {{para|talkpage}}, {{para|discuss}}, {{para|discussion}}) to give the location: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|talk{{=}}Talk:Topic#Talk page section|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} The parameter {{para|for}} can be used to give a {{em|short}} reason that displays in the tag (do not use this for editorializing, or else any other editor may refactor such a comment out); the comment added here will be preceded by "for:" and the entire comment put in a round-bracketed parenthetical: :{{Tlx|Disputed inline|Talk page section|for{{=}}conflict with sources cited above|date{{=}}{{CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{CURRENTYEAR}}}} '''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page''' and, as a general practice, should remain in place while the talk page discussion is active. ===Articles with disputed statements=== This inline template helps highlight a particular disputed statement or alleged fact, in contrast to the {{tlg|Disputed}} and {{tlg|Disputed section}} templates which mark a whole article or section as disputed. This is particularly helpful when there are reliable sources supporting two or more different claims. ''{{tlg|Disputed inline}} should not be used without also raising the issue on the talk page.'' When used on an article page, the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Disputed statement]], and the article is placed in [[:Category:Accuracy disputes]] or one of its dated sub-categories. This template is a more strongly worded version of {{tlg|Dubious}}, which indicates a potential dispute, most often a question about reliable sourcing for the statement/fact at issue; {{tlg|Disputed inline}} indicates that at least one editor believes there is no question that the statement has a [[WP:Verifiability|verifiability]] problem. ===Other pages=== If this tag is used outside the article namespace, it will not be categorized in an article cleanup category, and the word "disputed" links to [[Wikipedia:Consensus]]. It can be used to indicate an unresolved dispute and a talk page topic about the dispute, e.g. an issue at a guideline or WikiProject page. For pages other than articles, use {{tlg|Under discussion inline}}, which categorizes differently. == TemplateData== {{TemplateDataHeader}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Talk page section", "description": "Talk page section where the issue is discussed.", "type": "line", "suggested": true, "example": "Possibly incorrect facts" }, "for": { "description": "More info about the tag's inclusion.", "example": "Conflict between sources used" }, "talkpage": { "aliases": [ "talk", "discuss", "discussion" ], "description": "The page where the dispute is discussed.", "type": "wiki-page-name", "label": "A different discussion page?", "example": "Talk:Lorem ipsum#Possibly incorrect facts" }, "date": { "description": "Date issue was raised.", "example": "May 2016", "type": "date" } }, "description": "Inline template used to mark the content being disputed.", "format": "inline" } ==See also== * [[Template:Dispute templates]] {{inline tags}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Inline citation and verifiability dispute templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
